title: eriogonum elongatum
intro: Eriogonum elongatum, also known as the Mormon tea or desert trumpet, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing relief from digestive problems. Eriogonum elongatum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in herbal remedies to support overall health and well-being.
